JOURNAL (current thesis state):
# Journal — Investing Agent

## 1. Market view

Universe mixed-to-soft with unusual insider activity this week — Arnhult's Corem conviction buy opened the Aggressive sleeve for the first time. Cash at 70.8%, six positions across five sectors. Holding pattern into the July earnings cluster (Volvo, Essity, Thule all reporting Jul 23–29; ATRLJ ~Jul 10; CORE-B ~Jul 22). No urgency to deploy further before those prints resolve. Vimian and ASSA remain live but patient.

## 2. Holdings

**HM-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 5.0%
- **Thesis:** Karl-Johan Persson bought ~1.2 BSEK across six transactions in eight days into an 11% drawdown — among the strongest insider signals possible on Swedish Large Cap. Starter position pending fundamental confirmation.
- **Sell if:** Profit warning, gross margin collapse, or confirmed structural market-share loss to Shein/Temu. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ESSITY-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Staples | 8.0%
- **Thesis:** Exceptional 90-day insider cluster — 512 MSEK, 7 buyers, 0 sellers — led by Industrivärden. Defensive staples compounder at ~14x P/E with 3.4% yield. Q2 print ~Jul 24 is the next adjudicator.
- **Sell if:** Sustained margin deterioration where input costs structurally exceed pricing power, or Q2 shows revenue decline accelerating with margin compression. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**THULE.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 3.6%
- **Thesis:** Ten insiders including chair, vice chair, CEO, and CFO bought ~14–25 MSEK across two waves, zero sellers. Quality mid-cap franchise 28% off highs; weakness is cyclical not structural. Vice chairman's ~11.6 MSEK personal buy in mid-May confirms conviction post-drawdown.
- **Sell if:** Insider turns net seller, operating margin compresses below ~13%, or Q2 (~Jul 29) reveals genuine earnings deterioration rather than cyclical softness.
- **Status:** `intact`

**VOLV-B.ST** — Core | Industrials | 4.7%
- **Thesis:** Industrivärden deployed ~290 MSEK in a single day near 52-week highs. Net cash ~58 BSEK, ~4% yield, 9.8% operating margin despite -9.5% YoY revenue. Controlling owner buying near highs is a strong forward-earnings endorsement. Q2 ~Jul 24.
- **Sell if:** Industrivärden becomes net seller, profit warning, or Q2 reveals order intake collapse rather than cyclical normalisation. Revenue decline alone is not a sell trigger if margins hold.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ATRLJ-B.ST** — Core | Real Estate | 4.9%
- **Thesis:** Tagehus (controlling family) bought ~30 MSEK into Varma's forced 7%-discount block exit. Ericsson 58k sqm Hagastaden anchor lease provides fundamental confirmation. Starter size only given net debt/equity 101%.
- **Sell if:** Further forced-seller overhang materialises without insider response; Q2 (~Jul 10) reveals deteriorating occupancy/lease renewals; or Ericsson lease proves less economically material than signalled.
- **Status:** `intact`

**CORE-B.ST** — Aggressive | Real Estate | 3.0%
- **Thesis:** Rutger Arnhult (controlling owner/CEO) bought ~110 MSEK personally across two waves into a 50%-off, capitulating leveraged commercial real estate name — clearest Aggressive sleeve signal seen this cycle. FCF positive (678 MSEK R12); this is a leverage/rate-sensitivity story, not a going-concern story. Starter size only given net debt/equity 185%.
- **Sell if:** Dilutive equity raise announced, covenant breach disclosed, or Q2 (~Jul 22) reveals LTV deterioration or occupancy collapse. Arnhult becoming a net seller would be immediate exit.
- **Status:** `intact`

## 3. Watchlist

**VIMIAN.ST** — ~49 MSEK insider cluster (Fidelio Vet + CEO, Jun 12) on a quality animal-health compounder; +17.7% 3m recovery underway. Held back by P/E ~40 and negative FCF. Revisit after Q2 print ~Jul 29 before adding a second Aggressive position.

**ASSA-B.ST** — CEO bought ~35 MSEK across April and June on a quality compounder; stock at 332 vs 315 entry target. Q2 ~Jul 28. Watch for price reaching 315 SEK or post-print entry.

**NOLA-B.ST** — Quality industrial (10.8% op margin, clean balance sheet) at 23% drawdown with CEO transition overhang and shrinking revenue. Screener's "10.8 MSEK insider buy" was misleading — actual disclosed buy only 205k SEK. Revisit after Q2 ~Aug 5 and new CEO's first framing.

**LIFCO-B.ST** — April insider cluster (3 buyers, ~12 MSEK) still valid but stock rallied +10.5% 1m; original 270 SEK anchor stale. Revised entry zone 275–285 SEK. Revisit post-Q2 ~Jul 24 if price resets.

**SCST.ST** — Exceptional 8-buyer / 30 MSEK cluster including strategic peer Grupo Lusiaves. Still extended (+25% above 200MA). Revisit if price pulls back to ~135 SEK or post-Jul 28 earnings.

## 4. Lessons learned / open questions

- **Aggressive sleeve discipline:** CORE-B is the first use of the Aggressive sleeve — correctly sized small (~3%), entered on the clearest possible signal (controlling owner, personal capital, two waves). The sleeve exists for exactly this; the multi-week hesitation to open it was correct patience, not timidity.
- **Correlated real estate exposure:** Both ATRLJ and CORE-B are leveraged Swedish commercial real estate — partially correlated. Combined ~7.9%. Sector cap is 25% but concentration risk is real; no further real estate adds without rebalancing one of these.
- **Screener signal vs disclosed filing:** NOLA-B screener angle ("10.8 MSEK insider buy") did not match FI disclosure (205k SEK). Reinforces standing rule: cross-check every cluster signal against actual filings before acting. This is the same screener hygiene issue flagged last week.
- **Open question:** Four positions report in a 12-day window (ATRLJ ~Jul 10, CORE-B ~Jul 22, Volvo/Essity ~Jul 24, Thule ~Jul 29). Consider whether any pre-print trimming is warranted, or whether current sizes (all ≤8%) provide sufficient cushion across what is effectively one concentrated reporting season.

New-name insider signals (not currently held — you may open these):
  - HEXA-B.ST: significant insider buying in last 2 days — see Analyst note below
  - QLIRO.ST: significant insider buying in last 2 days — see Analyst note below
  - ASMDEE-B.ST: significant insider buying in last 2 days — see Analyst note below

ANALYST NOTES on new (not-yet-held) names surfaced today:
# Hexagon (HEXA-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis (2–3 sentences).** Hexagon has sold off hard (−19% over 3 months, −33% from 52-week high) into what looks like a coordinated cluster of insider buying from senior management — CFO, plus at least three "other management" buyers including repeated purchases by Andreas Wenzel in mid-June. The setup is classic: quality industrial software/sensor business under pressure, insiders stepping in with real money, and an earnings catalyst in ~5 weeks. Worth a closer look as a potential mean-reversion / inflection trade.

**Positives**
- Genuine insider cluster: 4 distinct buyers, zero sellers, ~12.4 MSEK net buy over 90 days, weighted toward the last six weeks as the price kept falling — that's accumulation into weakness, not casual signaling.
- CFO Patrickson buying ~3 MSEK on 2026-05-04 is the highest-signal name on the list; CFOs rarely buy at this size for show.
- Price is deeply extended below trend (−20% vs 200MA) on a high-quality large-cap industrial — the kind of drawdown that historically rewards patient entry if no thesis-breaking news is behind it.
- Recent bolt-on acquisition (ITRES, aerial mapping) suggests management is still executing on strategy, not in crisis mode.

**Concerns**
- I cannot see what caused the −19% 3-month drawdown. Without the news flow or the Q1 commentary in detail, I don't know whether this is sector rotation, a guidance cut, or something structural — that's a material gap.
- The fundamentals block shows values that look implausible for Hexagon (P/E 0.9, FCF yield 154%, div yield 20.6%, revenue 55bn EUR). These figures appear corrupted or unit-mismatched in the data feed; I cannot rely on them for valuation. **Treat the fundamentals line as unusable for this note.**
- R12 revenue YoY −5.9% (if directionally correct) suggests the business is in a soft patch — consistent with the price action.
- Volatility is elevated (30d vol 83%) — entries here need to be sized for chop, and earnings on ~2026-07-25 is a known binary event ~5 weeks out.

**Insider read.** This is one of the stronger insider signals I've seen — multi-buyer cluster, CFO included, buying *more* as the stock falls, zero sells. On a Swedish large-cap this matters; it says people who see the order book think the market is wrong.

**Price read.** Heavily oversold relative to 200MA and 52-week high, with volatility expanding. Classic "knife or bargain" tape — the insider cluster tilts it toward bargain, but I'd want either a stabilization signal or to size small and add post-earnings.

**Verdict.** The insider signal is strong enough to take seriously despite my data gaps; this fits the Core sleeve as a quality-industrial mean-reversion idea, but PM should be aware I'm flying partially blind on the cause of the drawdown.

`INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve`

Fundamentals block in my input looked unreliable; please cross-check before sizing. Insider signal here is unusually clean (CFO + 3 managers, all buys, accelerating into weakness)."
```json
}
```

# Qliro (QLIRO.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Qliro is a small-cap Swedish fintech lender in the middle of a leadership reset, and the newly installed CEO Christoffer Rutgersson has been buying shares in size on the open market this week — a concrete, personal-capital vote of confidence in his own turnaround plan. The setup is a classic "new CEO + insider cluster on a beaten-down name" pattern, but the underlying business is still loss-making and the price hasn't broken out, so this is a thesis about *future* execution, not present quality.

**Positives**
- CEO open-market buys on 2026-06-16 are substantial and concentrated on a single day across multiple tickets (~1.1 MSEK gross buy that day alone), right after he took the role — this is high-signal insider behavior on a small cap.
- 90-day insider picture is net buy ~2.25 MSEK with 3 buyers vs 2 sellers; the signal is broader than one person.
- Balance sheet shows zero net debt, which buys time for the turnaround — the company isn't being squeezed by lenders while it fixes operations.
- Stock is down 26% from the 52-week high and roughly flat against the 200-day MA, so expectations are low and the entry isn't chasing strength.

**Concerns**
- Fundamentals are poor: operating margin -20%, net margin -19%, ROE -14%, FCF -162 MSEK (yield -30%). This is a loss-making lender, not a quality compounder.
- 3-year revenue CAGR is negative (-1.3%); the top line isn't even growing into the losses.
- I cannot evaluate credit quality, loan book composition, or funding mix from the data I have — for a consumer-credit fintech, that is the *core* question and it's a real blind spot.
- The CEO also recorded several SELL tickets on the same day as the buys (~1.08 MSEK gross sell). Net is still a buy, but the optics are messier than a clean accumulation — possibly portfolio reshuffling, tax, or program-adjacent activity I can't disentangle from the feed.
- Next earnings ~2026-08-11 is the real test; until then the turnaround is a story, not a number.

**Insider read.** Clearly net positive: a new CEO putting personal money to work in size within days of being interviewed about his turnaround focus is the strongest qualitative insider signal available. The simultaneous sell tickets dampen the signal somewhat but don't reverse it — net buy across multiple buyers stands.

**Price read.** 18.90 SEK is mid-range — 26% off the 52-week high, just above the 200-day MA, mild recent weakness (-3.8% 1m). Not a breakout, not a capitulation. A reasonable level to start a position if one wanted exposure, with room to add lower.

**Verdict.** This is a speculative turnaround on a loss-making lender with a strong fresh insider signal but weak fundamentals and a key data blind spot (credit quality). It does not belong in Core. It is a plausible Aggressive sleeve candidate, but conviction must be modest — I'd want to see the August Q2 print before sizing up.

**Verdict: INTERESTING — fits Aggressive sleeve**

Next earnings ~2026-08-11 is the gating event; if PM enters, sizing should leave room to add or exit after that print. Insider signal is the main reason to act now rather than wait; same-day CEO sell tickets exist alongside the buys so the signal is good but not pristine."
```json
}
```

# Asmodee (ASMDEE-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Asmodee, the board games / tabletop publisher (Catan, Ticket to Ride, Star Wars: Unlimited), posted a strong Q4 report on May 21 with +24% revenue growth and a dividend proposal, and the CEO just bought ~1.2 MSEK of stock on the open market four weeks later. That combo — strong print, market liking it (+31% over 3m), and a CEO follow-on buy at elevated prices — is a credible "things are working" signal worth investigating.

**Positives**
- CEO Thomas Koegler bought 1.2 MSEK on 2026-06-17, after the stock had already rallied 31% in three months — buying strength rather than weakness, which is the stronger version of the insider signal.
- Strong fundamental print: R12 revenue +23.9% YoY, gross margin 42.5%, and reported FCF of 2,304 MEUR; even if some of that FCF figure reflects working-capital effects from a recent spin/listing, the order of magnitude suggests genuine cash generation.
- Headline P/E of 9.4 is undemanding for a 20%+ growing consumer franchise with branded IP — *if* the earnings are durable.
- Three independent outlets flagged the Q4 report positively; the market reaction was constructive.

**Concerns**
- Net margin is only 1.6% against gross of 42.5% and operating of 10% — that gap implies large below-the-line items (interest, one-offs, amortization from the Embracer spin). EPS of 1.30 and ROE of 1.5% suggest reported earnings are depressed, so the "P/E 9.4" is potentially misleading until I can see clean run-rate earnings.
- FCF of 2,304 MEUR against revenue of 18,421 MEUR (12.5% FCF margin) and an 80.6% FCF yield looks too good to be true — almost certainly distorted by spin-off mechanics or a one-time working capital release. I cannot verify the underlying cash conversion from the data I have.
- Net debt of 2,818 MEUR is material; needs context on covenants and maturity profile that I don't have.
- The stock is +31% in 3m and only -12% from the 52w high with 43% vol — entry timing is not favorable; chasing into a post-earnings rally is exactly what the constitution warns against.
- Tabletop/hobby games is a category with hit-driven cyclicality (Magic, Pokémon comps); I cannot assess the franchise pipeline from the data I have.

**Insider read.** CEO open-market buy of 1.2 MSEK at elevated prices, post-strong-print, is a genuine positive — not equity-program noise. One buyer, no sellers in 90 days. Solid but not a cluster.

**Price read.** Px 134.40, +15.9% above 200dma, +31% over 3m, -12% from 52w high. Momentum is clearly with it; the easy money on the report is gone. This is not a discounted entry.

**Verdict.** The CEO buy + strong print combination is real, but the headline valuation metrics need scrubbing before I can underwrite a thesis, and the entry point is post-rally. Put on the watchlist and wait for either (a) a pullback toward the 200dma (~116) or (b) the next quarterly print to confirm earnings quality.

`WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist`

```json
}
```

RISK CAPS (mirrors constitution §4 — enforced in code):
  - Max 15% single holding (of total portfolio)
  - Max 25% any single sector (of total)
  - Max 10% single Aggressive position
  - Max 20% Aggressive sleeve total
  - Min 30% cash buffer of the Core sleeve
  - Max 90% total equity exposure
  - Max ~10 holdings
  - Min 4-week holding period — early full exit requires thesis_break=True + rationale
response_text:
**Trigger read.** Four signals fired. VIMIAN: two small insider sales (~2 MSEK) partially offset the June 12 cluster — weakens conviction modestly but doesn't break it; we don't hold it anyway. HEXA-B: strong multi-buyer cluster including CFO into a 33% drawdown, but analyst is "flying partially blind" on the cause and fundamentals data is corrupted. QLIRO: new-CEO buy, but loss-making lender with credit-quality blind spot — conviction 2. ASMDEE-B: explicitly "WATCH, don't chase" — post-rally entry, distorted fundamentals.

**Decision.**

*HEXA-B.ST — pass for now.* This is the most tempting of the three: clean insider cluster, CFO included, accumulating into weakness on a quality industrial. But the analyst flagged two material gaps — unknown cause of the −19% drawdown and unusable fundamentals data. The constitution is explicit: "When the data is insufficient, say so." Sunday is four days away; that's enough time to get a clean read on what's driving the sell-off and verify the fundamentals before sizing. The Q1 earnings on Jul 25 is still ~5 weeks out — there's no time pressure that beats the cost of acting half-blind on a Core-sleeve large-cap. Wait for the weekly cycle with a proper dossier.

*QLIRO.ST — pass.* Conviction 2, loss-making, credit-quality blind spot on a consumer lender. The CEO buy is real but small (~1.1 MSEK gross) and accompanied by same-day sell tickets. CORE-B already uses ~3% of the 20% Aggressive budget; opening a second speculative Aggressive name on conviction 2 with the August earnings as the real gate is not a decision that has to be made today. Revisit Sunday with cleaner work.

*ASMDEE-B.ST — pass.* Analyst said WATCH explicitly. +31% in 3m, distorted FCF metrics, no business chasing.

*VIMIAN.ST — no action, note for journal.* We don't hold it. The watchlist conviction weakens slightly; Journal Keeper should note the offsetting sales on Sunday's rewrite.

**Risk awareness.** Cash 70.9%, Aggressive 2.9% of 20% cap, Real Estate 7.8% of 25%. Nothing tight. Plenty of room to act Sunday if any of these survive a second look.

Bottom line: three insider triggers fired, none rise above the "this can't wait until Sunday" bar. The weekly cycle exists precisely so we don't have to act on partial information.

```json
{
  "summary": "No action — HEXA-B is the most interesting signal but has material data gaps; better to dossier it for Sunday than enter half-blind. QLIRO and ASMDEE do not clear the intra-week bar.",
  "trades": [],
  "no_action_note": "Three insider triggers (HEXA-B, QLIRO, ASMDEE-B) and one weakening watchlist signal (VIMIAN). HEXA-B is the strongest case but the analyst flagged unknown cause of the drawdown and corrupted fundamentals data — acting today would be speculation. Sunday is four days away with the same names still available; the weekly cycle is the right venue for a proper underwrite. Q2 earnings cluster for held names is still 3+ weeks out, no thesis breaks today."
}
```
